segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, electronic track as a calm, professional female voice welcomes the audience to OpenAI Dev Day and introduces Sam Altman to the stage. A man, identified as Sam Altman, in a dark green long-sleeve sweater and dark pants, walks confidently onto the dimly lit stage, holding a small black device, as the audience erupts in loud applause and cheers. The stage's multiple screens initially display 'OPENAI DEV DAY' on a light pink background. As the camera zooms in on Sam, revealing his focused yet casual demeanor, he greets the audience in a clear, confident voice, expressing excitement for the event and the 'awesome' energy, which prompts another wave of enthusiastic applause and cheers. He welcomes everyone to San Francisco, highlighting its importance to OpenAI and the tech industry. Sam then announces 'some great stuff to announce today,' but first, he recounts the past year's developments, mentioning that about a year ago, on November 30th, they 'shipped ChatGPT as a low-key research preview,' which 'went pretty well,' eliciting chuckles from the audience. During his speech, the background screens transition to a dark, pixelated pattern, displaying text about ChatGPT's viral success ('December 1st, 2022 — Evan Morikawa 11:55 AM: ChatGPT went viral. 100k people have tried this so far.'), followed by a side-by-side comparison of '⚡ GPT-3.5' and '✨ GPT-4', towards which Sam gestures, emphasizing the technological advancements.

As he approaches the center, the camera zooms in to a medium close-up, revealing his focused expression and casual yet professional demeanor. The lighting remains focused on him, with the audience in shadow, creating a sense of intimacy and focus on the presentation. The atmosphere is professional, tech-forward, and anticipatory, as the speaker introduces key developments in OpenAI’s technology.

segments.integrated_caption: The Speaker, a man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater, confidently presents on a dimly lit stage, announcing the launch of GPT-4 as "the most capable model out in the world." Behind him, a large screen displays smartphone interfaces, one showing a voice assistant with a 'Tap to interrupt' button and the other a ChatGPT conversation. A large, shadowed audience erupts in loud applause, acknowledging the announcement. The Speaker continues, explaining that in recent months, they launched "voice and vision capabilities" allowing ChatGPT to "see, hear, and speak," as he gestures towards the screen. The audience claps again, prompting the Speaker to humorously interject, "There's a lot, you don't have to clap each time." The screen then transitions to a DALL-E interface, demonstrating a user requesting an image of a seal juggling, as the Speaker announces the launch of DALL-E 3, "the world's most advanced image model," which can be used "inside of ChatGPT." Moving to stand beside a wooden podium with a closed MacBook, the Speaker introduces "ChatGPT Enterprise" for business customers, detailing its "enterprise-grade security and privacy, higher speed GPT-4 access, longer context windows, lot more," while the screen displays the 'ChatGPT Enterprise' title alongside logos of major companies like AMGEN and Lowe’s. As the Speaker gestures, the screen sequentially highlights impressive statistics: "2 million developers" building on their API, "over 92% of Fortune 500 companies" utilizing their products, and "100 million weekly active users," each appearing in distinct colored boxes. The warm stage lighting continues to focus on the Speaker, emphasizing the presentation's content.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the Speaker, a man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater, confidently on stage, concluding a point about "chat GPT" to a round of applause. He emphasizes that their success came "entirely through word of mouth," as people "find it useful and tell their friends." As he speaks, gesturing expressively and maintaining direct eye contact, the Presentation Slides behind him evolve, first showing 'Developers' on a light green background, then transitioning to 'Fortune 500' on a brown background, reinforcing his points about OpenAI being "the most advanced and the most widely used AI platform in the world now." He then states that "numbers never tell the whole picture" and that "what's really important is how people use the products," before introducing a "quick video." The scene cuts to a video call interface, introducing Risa, an Artist with long black hair and large black-framed glasses, speaking from a warmly lit room. She explains in a clear voice how she "wanted to write something to my dad in Tagalog," seeking "a non-romantic way to tell my parent that I love him" while also conveying reliance and maintaining "the respect of like a child to parent relationship" within Filipino culture and Tagalog grammar. She then quotes the translated message: "I love you very deeply and I will be with you no matter where the path leads." The video then transitions to Christian, a Founder with a short beard, who smiles and gestures with both hands, concluding the segment by stating in Spanish, "Así son las posibilidades de la IA" (These are the possibilities of AI).

segments.integrated_caption: A man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater and dark pants, the Speaker, stands confidently on a stage, holding a small black remote and gesturing expressively. Behind him, a large screen initially shows a large 'O' before transitioning to a grid of six colored squares, with 'Context length' appearing under square 01. The Speaker begins by discussing the limitations of a "2K context length" before enthusiastically announcing that "GPT-4 Turbo supports up to 128,000 tokens of context," which is met with loud, enthusiastic applause from the shadowed audience, some of whom are recording with their phones. He elaborates that this represents "300 pages of a standard book, 16 times longer" and that the model is "much more accurate." As the screen changes to a pattern of white document icons and then back to the grid, now highlighting square 02 with the text 'More control', the Speaker transitions to "Number two: More control," emphasizing that "developers need more control over the model's responses." The presentation then shifts to a JSON code block showing weather data with 'JSON Mode: ON' toggled, as the Speaker introduces "JSON mode" as a "huge developer request" for ensuring valid JSON responses. Finally, a function calling demonstration appears, showing a user's request 'raise my windows and turn the radio on' processed more efficiently, with the Speaker explaining the model's improved ability to call "many functions at once" and follow instructions. The focused lighting maintains a professional and dynamic atmosphere throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a man, the Speaker, in a dark green sweater, holding a small black device, speaking expressively against a black backdrop. He immediately begins discussing "where we're headed" and the main topic of the day, stating, "We believe that if you give people better tools, they will do amazing things." He elaborates on the desire for AI that is "smarter, more personal, more customizable, can do more on your behalf," envisioning a future where one can simply ask a computer for what they need, and it will perform "all of these tasks." As the camera occasionally zooms out, it reveals the large, dark stage setup with a wooden panel and a shadowed audience, some recording with phones, captivated by his words. The Speaker introduces the concept of AI "agents," emphasizing that "the upsides of this are going to be tremendous." He explicitly states, "At OpenAI, we really believe that gradual iterative deployment is the best way to address the safety issues, the safety challenges with AI." He stresses the importance of moving carefully towards this future, acknowledging it will require "a lot of technical work and a lot of thoughtful consideration by society." Towards the end, the screen behind him transitions to display the bold white 'ChatGPT' logo, followed by a grid of app icons like 'Pun Generator', 'Code Checker', 'Study Buddy', and 'Golf Tracker', visually illustrating the AI applications he's discussing. He gestures towards the screen, concluding by announcing, "So today, we're taking our first small step that moves us towards this future. We're thrilled to introduce..." as the segment ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the Speaker, a man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater and dark pants, standing on a large stage under focused lighting. Behind him, a dark backdrop displays a grid of colorful app icons like 'Pun Generator', 'Code Checker', and 'Study Buddy' under the 'ChatGPT' logo. The audience, visible as shadowed silhouettes, is engaged, with some recording on their phones. The screen then transitions to a minimalist black background with the bold white text 'GPTs' centered, as the Speaker clearly states, "GPTs." He continues, explaining that "GPTs are tailored versions of ChatGPT for a specific purpose," holding a small black device and gesturing expressively with his left hand. The camera zooms in, capturing his animated movements and focused expression. As he elaborates, "You can build a GPT, a customized version of ChatGPT, for almost anything. With instructions, expanded knowledge, and actions," the screen changes again to display three colored panels: 'Instructions' in pink, 'Expanded knowledge' in dark teal, and 'Actions' in mustard yellow, which he gestures towards. The Speaker emphasizes that "because they combine instructions, expanded knowledge, and actions, they can be more helpful to you," working better in many contexts and offering better control. Alternating between wide shots of the stage and close-ups, he maintains an engaging presence, shifting his stance and using precise hand gestures to underscore his points. He adds that GPTs "make it easier for you to accomplish all sorts of tasks or just have more fun," and can be used "right within ChatGPT." He further clarifies, "You can in effect program a GPT with language, just by talking to it. It's easy to customize the behavior so that it fits what you want. This makes building them very accessible and it gives agency to everyone." The lighting consistently highlights him against the dark background, keeping the audience in shadow. He concludes by outlining the presentation's agenda: "So, we're going to show you what GPTs are, how to use them, how to build them, and then we're going to talk about how they'll be distributed and discovered."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Speaker, a man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater and dark pants, holding a small black device, gesturing expressively against a black background as he announces, "for developers, we're going to show you how to build these agent-like experiences into your own apps." He then invites the audience to "look at a few examples." The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large stage with a dark backdrop, where the Speaker is now on the right, explaining that "Our partners at Code.org are working hard to expand computer science in schools." Behind him, a large screen displays the 'Code.org Lesson Planner' interface, featuring its logo and the tagline 'Making computer science fun and engaging.' He continues, noting Code.org's curriculum is "used by tens of millions of students worldwide" and that they "crafted Lesson Planner GPT to help teachers provide a more engaging experience for middle schoolers." The screen updates to show a conversation within the Code.org Lesson Planner, illustrating how "If a teacher asks it to explain four loops in a creative way, it does just that," by using a video game character analogy for "repeatedly picking up coins," which is "super easy to understand for an eighth grader." The Speaker emphasizes that "this GPT brings together Code.org's extensive curriculum and expertise and lets teachers adapt it to their needs quickly and easily." The screen then switches to the 'Canva GPT' interface, displaying its logo and tagline 'Create anything in a snap, from presentations and logos to social media posts and more,' as the Speaker introduces it, stating "Canva has built a GPT that lets you start designing by describing what you want in natural language." He gives an example, "If you say, make a poster for DevDay," as the screen shows two generated vertical poster options for a DevDay reception. The Speaker continues to gesture toward the posters while the attentive audience, some recording with phones, watches from the foreground under focused stage lighting.

The system responds by suggesting 'Startup Mentor' as a name, and a profile picture is generated. He concludes the demonstration by saying, "So that's what it looks like to create a GPT." As he steps away from the podium, holding a small black device, the audience erupts in loud applause and cheering, to which the Speaker responds, "Thank you." He then speaks to the audience, gesturing with his hands, sharing, "By the way, I always, I always wanted to do that after like all of the YC office hours, I always thought, man, someday I will be able to make a bot that will do this and that'll be awesome." The Speaker continues to speak center stage, with the audience visible in shadowed rows in the foreground, some holding up phones to record. He explains, "So, with GPTs, we're letting people easily share and discover all the fun ways that they use ChatGPT with the world," adding that "You can make private GPTs, like I just did," before the audio cuts off as he mentions sharing.

Finally, the screen displays a colorful icon labeled 'Threading', depicting interconnected blocks, as the Speaker continues his presentation with focused gestures and a calm demeanor, beginning to explain what the Assistants API includes.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sam, a man in a dark green long-sleeve sweater and dark pants, standing on a large stage with a dark backdrop and wooden paneling. He gestures expressively while speaking clearly and professionally about 'persistent threads,' 'built-in retrieval,' 'code interpreter' (a working Python interpreter in a sandbox environment), and 'improved function calling,' all of which are displayed with corresponding icons on a massive screen behind him. The audience, visible as silhouettes, watches attentively, some recording with their phones. Sam then announces, "So we'd like to show you a demo of how this works, and here is Ramon, our head of developer experience. Welcome, Ramon!" As Sam exits stage left, upbeat, energetic background music plays, accompanied by audience applause. A new presenter, Ramon, a man in a dark blue long-sleeve shirt and light jeans, walks onto the stage from the right, waving to the cheering crowd. He approaches a wooden podium with a laptop, thanks Sam, and enthusiastically greets the audience with a slight accent, saying, "Good morning! Wow. It's fantastic to see you all here." Ramon expresses how inspiring it is to see AI infused into apps and announces new API modalities and improved developer experience for building assistive agents, concluding, "So let's dive right in."
